There are approximately 600 million domestic cats in the world, making them one of the most popular pets on the planet.
Cats were first domesticated around 10,000 years ago in the Near East, likely when wildcats were attracted to rodents in grain stores.
A group of cats is called a clowder, while a group of kittens is called a kindle.
Cats spend approximately 70% of their lives sleeping, which is up to 16 hours a day for most adults.
A cat's nose print is as unique as a human fingerprint, and no two cats have the same pattern.
Cats have 32 muscles in each ear and can rotate them 180 degrees to precisely locate sounds.
Domestic cats can run at speeds of up to 30 miles per hour (48 km/h) in short bursts.
Cats have a third eyelid called a nictitating membrane that helps protect and moisten the eye.
A cat's heart beats roughly twice as fast as a human heart, at between 140 and 220 beats per minute.
Cats are obligate carnivores, meaning they cannot survive on a vegetarian diet, as they require nutrients only found in animal protein.
Cats cannot taste sweetness because they lack the taste receptor gene that detects sugar.
A cat has five toes on its front paws but only four on its back paws, unless it is a polydactyl cat.
Polydactyl cats (cats with extra toes) are sometimes called 'Hemingway cats' because the writer kept a colony of them.
The oldest cat on record was Creme Puff, a tabby from Austin, Texas, who lived to be 38 years and 3 days old.
Cats have 230 bones in their body, which is 24 more than humans.
A cat's collarbone is free-floating, allowing them to squeeze through any opening their head can fit through.
Cats have 473 taste buds, compared to humans' 9,000, but their olfactory system more than compensates.
Cats can make over 100 different vocal sounds, while dogs can make only about 10.
The scientific name for the domestic cat is Felis catus.
Cats are the only mammals known to not taste sweetness, due to a mutation in their taste receptor genes.
A cat's brain is biologically more similar to a human brain than a dog's brain is.
Cats spend up to 50% of their waking hours grooming themselves.
The average cat has 24 whiskers, arranged in 4 rows on each side of the nose.
Cats' whiskers (vibrissae) are deeply rooted sensory organs that can detect changes in air pressure and space.
Cats can jump up to 6 times their own body length in a single leap.
A cat's hearing range extends from 48 Hz to 85,000 Hz, far beyond both human and dog hearing.
Cats have a specialized organ in the roof of their mouth called the Jacobson's organ (also known as the vomeronasal organ) to analyze scents.
The flehmen response, that wide-mouthed grimace cats sometimes make, helps draw air over the Jacobson's organ.
Cats have a 'righting reflex' that allows them to orient themselves during a fall and land on their feet most of the time.
The world's first cat video was made by Thomas Edison in 1894, featuring two cats appearing to box each other.
Cats were worshipped as gods in ancient Egypt, and the goddess Bastet was depicted as a cat or cat-headed woman.
Killing a cat in ancient Egypt was punishable by death.
The Egyptian Mau is one of the oldest domesticated cat breeds, with a history stretching back thousands of years.
Cats mark their territory by scratching, which both leaves a visual mark and deposits scent from glands in their paw pads.
A cat's tongue has tiny backward-facing hooks (papillae) made of keratin, the same material as human fingernails.
The cat's tongue acts as a highly efficient self-cleaning comb, removing dirt and parasites from fur.
Cats have scent glands on their cheeks, forehead, chin, base of the tail, and between their toe pads.
The world's most expensive cat breed is the Ashera, which can cost up to $100,000.
The world's smallest cat breed is the Singapura, with adults weighing as little as 4 pounds.
Cats use their tails for balance, communication, and expressing emotion, making the tail a critical feline communication tool.
Domestic cats share 95.6% of their DNA with tigers.
Cats have a specialized 'tapetum lucidum' layer behind their retina that reflects light and enhances night vision.
A cat's pupils can open to the size of almost the entire visible eye in low light, letting in maximum illumination.
Cats can see clearly in one-sixth of the light that humans need to see.
The cat's field of view is about 200 degrees, compared to 180 degrees for humans.
Cats are red-green color blind but can see blues and yellows more clearly than humans can.
Cats are crepuscular, meaning they are most active at dawn and dusk, and are not truly nocturnal.
Cats instinctively bury their waste to hide their scent from predators, and this behavior carries over to litter box use.
Research shows that cats prefer their owner's voice over strangers' voices, even if they don't always respond.
Cats can drink seawater safely, as their kidneys are able to filter out salt that would dehydrate a human.
The cat's ability to land on its feet is called the 'righting reflex' and involves the vestibular system in the inner ear.
Spaying or neutering a cat significantly increases its lifespan, and indoor neutered cats can live up to 20 years.
Black cats are considered good luck in Japan, Scotland, and England, and the bad luck association is largely an American and Western European superstition.
There are over 70 recognized domestic cat breeds worldwide according to various cat registries.
The tabby coat pattern is caused by the agouti gene and is one of the most ancient and widespread patterns in felines.
All orange cats have the tabby gene, which is why you can always see faint tabby stripes on an orange cat even if it is not a tabby.
Almost all calico and tortoiseshell cats are female due to the genetics of coat color being linked to the X chromosome.
Male calico cats occur in roughly 1 in 3,000 calico births and are almost always sterile.
The purring frequency of a cat (25-50 Hz) is within the range scientifically proven to promote bone healing.
Cats have been shown to reduce the risk of heart attack in their owners by up to 40%, according to a University of Minnesota study.
Cats were brought aboard ships for centuries to control rats and mice, and 'ship cats' were considered good luck by sailors.
The first cat in space was Felicette, a French cat who was launched into space on October 18, 1963, and returned safely.
A cat's memory is about 200 times better than a dog's, and they can recall experiences and people for years.
Cats knead with their paws (sometimes called 'making biscuits') as a soothing behavior rooted in kittenhood nursing.
The world record for the longest domestic cat is held by Barivel, a Maine Coon from Italy measuring 3 feet 11.2 inches.
Cats are the most popular pet in the world by number, and there are more pet cats than pet dogs globally.
National Cat Day is celebrated on October 29 in the United States and was founded in 2005 by animal welfare advocate Colleen Paige.
World Cat Day is celebrated on August 8 and was created by the International Fund for Animal Welfare in 2002.
The domestic cat is the only cat species that holds its tail vertically while walking, as wild cats hold their tails horizontally or tucked.
Cats have a unique stride in which they move both legs on one side of their body at the same time, a gait called 'direct registering.'
Cats are extraordinarily adept at detecting earthquakes before they happen, likely due to their sensitivity to vibration and infrasound.
A cat named Stubbs served as the honorary mayor of Talkeetna, Alaska, for nearly 20 years.
The average lifespan of an indoor cat is 12 to 18 years, while outdoor cats typically live only 2 to 5 years.
Cats have an extremely sensitive digestive system, and sudden food changes can cause vomiting and diarrhea.
A cat's skeleton is so flexible because the spine contains 30 vertebrae, which is 5 more than humans have.
Cats have specialized pads on their paws that are highly sensitive to texture, pressure, and vibration.
The domestic cat's closest wild ancestor is the African wildcat (Felis silvestris lybica), which still lives in Africa and the Middle East.
Cats can rotate their ears independently to track two different sound sources at once.
When cats meow, they are almost always communicating with humans, as adult cats rarely meow at each other.
Each cat develops a unique meow vocabulary tailored to communicate effectively with their specific human family.
The largest domestic cat breed is the Maine Coon, with males sometimes reaching 25 pounds.
Cats are tactile communicators, and a cat touching noses with another cat or person is an expression of trust and affection.
The Persian cat has been the most popular cat breed in the United States for decades.
Cats have a specialized structure in their throat that allows them to both purr and produce other vocalizations.
Unlike dogs, cats do not have a 'pack mentality,' as they are solitary hunters by nature, which shapes much of their behavior.
Despite being solitary hunters, cats can form strong social bonds with other cats, especially when raised together from kittenhood.
Cats have a natural hunting sequence of stalk, chase, pounce, and kill bite, and play toys that allow them to complete this sequence are best.
The first cat show was held at Crystal Palace in London in 1871, organized by Harrison Weir.
A cat named Nyan Cat became one of the most iconic internet memes of all time, spawning millions of views and variations.
Cats can recognize their own name but choose whether or not to respond, which is a hallmark of their independent nature.
Research from 2019 confirmed that cats understand object permanence, meaning they know an object exists even when they cannot see it.
Cats have been shown to exhibit empathy by adjusting their behavior in response to their owner's emotional state.
Cats in multi-cat households develop complex social hierarchies and communication systems among themselves.
A 2020 study found that, given the choice, cats prefer social interaction with humans over food, toys, and scent.
Cats are so sensitive to vibration that they can feel a person walking across the room through their paw pads.
The richest cat in history was Blackie, who inherited 15 million pounds (approximately $25 million) from its British owner Ben Rea in 1988.
